conditions.description: Participation is limited to legal entities established in EU Member States, Iceland, Norway, and the following associated countries: Canada, Israel, Republic of Korea, New Zealand, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.
Entities controlled by non-eligible countries may be excluded unless appropriate guarantees are provided.
Activities are expected to start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.
Maximum EU contribution per project is €19,000,000; minimum contribution is also set at €19,000,000, making this a fixed-amount funding topic.

Financial support to third parties (FSTP) is permitted with the following limits: maximum €300,000 per grant at stage 2; maximum €2,250,000 per grant at stage 3; maximum €2,550,000 per individual third party.
Standard minimum consortium composition requirements for RIA actions apply: at least 3 independent legal entities from at least 3 different EU Member States or associated countries.
conditions.inference_note: The call identifies this as a 'Research and Innovation Actions' (RIA) type action. Standard Horizon Europe RIA minimum consortium composition requirements (at least 3 independent legal entities from at least 3 different Member States or associated countries) are not explicitly stated in the source extract documents but are inferred as applicable from the RIA instrument type designation. Verification against Tier 1 normative sources (General Annexes) is required.

conditions.assumption_note: The 48-month maximum project duration is not explicitly stated in the call topic text. The value is drawn from the selected_call.json binding metadata field max_project_duration_months, which itself carries a note that 48 months is the standard maximum for large RIA actions and requires verification against General Annex B. This assumption must be confirmed against Tier 1 normative sources before finalising the project duration in the proposal.

conditions.description: Open access to research outputs is required as a standard Horizon Europe programme condition applicable to all funded RIA actions; no derogation is indicated in the call extract.
conditions.assumption_note: Open access requirements are not explicitly stated in the call extract source documents. They are a standard Horizon Europe programme condition applicable to all RIA-type funded actions. The call extract does not indicate any derogation from standard open access requirements. This assumption requires verification against Tier 1 normative sources (General Annexes and Annotated Grant Agreement).
